title: TaqMan Genotyping Assay for Detection of B.1.1.7 SARS-CoV-2
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"925to","text":"At the end of 2020, several new lineages of SARS-CoV-2 were reported that may be more transmissible, less responsive to antibody therapy, or able to “escape” the protection of recently approved vaccines, including B.1.1.7, first reported in the United Kingdom (1). Certain mutations affect functionally important parts of viral proteins and are termed “variants of concern” or VOC. The existing SARS-CoV-2 qPCR and antigen tests cannot identify these new viral variants. Advanced molecular diagnostic assays are needed to understand the spread of VOCs in the state. Viral genome sequencing is the gold standard for characterization mutations, lineage classification, and high-resolution genomic surveillance, but only a small proportion of isolates are currently analyzed by this approach in the USA. Faster and simpler approaches to identifying VOCs based on assays targeting specific mutations represent a complementary approach for monitoring known lineages using assays. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bl655","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"defbq","text":"The B.1.1.7 strain exhibits 17 characteristic mutations including a del69-70 mutation in the S-gene binding domain. This mutation can be monitored using S-gene target of the Applied Biosystems TaqPath™ COVID-19 qPCR assay. Samples that are positive for the N-gene and Orf1ab targets of the multiplexed TaqPath™ assay with weak or no S-gene signal are classified as S-gene target failure (SGTF) samples. The del69-70 mutation has been observed in multiple variant lineages and is not unique to B.1.1.7. We report a set of TaqMan™ genotyping assays to identify B.1.1.7 cases based on discrimination between wild-type and mutant alleles that encode three variants in the spike protein. The genotyping assays described here can be used as follow-up assays to determine whether samples share other characteristic mutations that are more indicative of the B.1.1.7 strain – S.A570D and S.D1118H – as well as the S.N501Y mutation that is shared by many VOCs. The targeted genotyping approach can be performed in most labs that conduct SARS-CoV-2 testing using RT-PCR. Broad use of this and similar approaches can provide an expanded perspective on the prevalence of B.1.1.7 at lower cost and faster turn-around time than whole genome sequencing.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bd7gf","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3h8sl","text":"(1) https://virological.org/t/preliminary-genomic-characterisation-of-an-emergent-sars-cov-2-lineage-in-the-uk-defined-by-a-novel-set-of-spike-mutations/563","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":4,"length":152}],"data":{}},{"key":"2b8lo","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"722592AC7CFE11EBB79F0A58A9FEAC2A","url":"https://virological.org/t/preliminary-genomic-characterisation-of-an-emergent-sars-cov-2-lineage-in-the-uk-defined-by-a-novel-set-of-spike-mutations/563"}}}}

Wuhan-Hu-1) and Cat# 103907 (Control 14; England/205041766/2020 as a B.1.1.7 representative) ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fboln","text":"Molecular Grade Water (RNase Free)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cpst1","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cgokk","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f25a1","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b73go","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"g2l7","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"tables","mutability":"","data":{"cellsMeta":{},"colTitles":["","",""],"colWidths":[100,100,100],"data":[["Base","Amino Acid","Assay ID"],["A_23063_T","N501Y","ANGZU27"],["C_23271_A","A570D","ANNK6DJ"],["G_24914_C","D1118H","ANPRYXG"]],"guid":"CFAED55C7C5811EB89FB0A58A9FEAC2A","isJexcelDataFormat":true,"legend":{"blocks":[{"data":{},"depth":0,"entityRanges":[],"inlineStyleRanges":[],"key":"cjelv","text":"TaqMan Custom Genotyping Assays","type":"unstyled"}],"entityMap":{}},"mergeCells":{},"printData":[["Base","Amino Acid","Assay ID"],["A_23063_T","N501Y","ANGZU27"],["C_23271_A","A570D","ANNK6DJ"],["G_24914_C","D1118H","ANPRYXG"]],"rowHeights":[23,23,23,23]}}}}
steps: Gather Supplies Needed for the Assay as listed in the Materials tab and: 384-Well PCR Plate Optical Grade PCR plate adhesive film seal 384-Well cold block Ice Bucket (ice) Pipettes and Tips for p20 and p200 pipettes
Locate and thaw on ice the extracted RNA samples to be tested.
Label a sterile PCR Tube for each sample to be tested.
Aliquot 20uL of Molecular Grade water into each labeled tube.
Add 5uL of Extracted RNA into each labeled tube and triturate to mix. (avoid bubbles)
Label a sterile PCR Tube for each Allele of Interest. Keep on ice during preparation of Maser Mix.

For 10 ul reactions performed in a 384-well plate, calculate the number of reactions to be performed including the controls and prepare a Master Mix for each Allele using the following components TaqMan Custom Primer/Probe mixes are shipped as 40x stocks. Dilute the stocks to a 20x working stock with sterile 1x TE buffer. Store the multiple aliquots at -15C to -25C in the dark. Thaw on ice prior to use.
Aliquot out 5.5uL of each Allele specific Master Mix into a Specific Quadrant of the 384 well plate for all samples.

Aliquot 5.5uL of each Allele specific Master Mix into a Specific Quadrant of the 384 well plate for controls in Columns 23 and 24, leave columns 21 and 22 empty to avoid control contamination if possible.
Add 4.5uL of Diluted Sample to each well containing Allele Specific Master Mix

Add 4.5uL of Wuhan-Hu-1 control (WT), B.1.1.7 control (VAR), or a 50:50 mixture of the two controls to serve as a "heterozygous" equivalent control (100c/ul) for each Allele specific Master Mix. Use Molecular Grade Water as a no-template control.
Seal the PCR plate with an optical adhesive film Vortex the plate for 3 seconds Spin down the plate at 2000rpm for 1 minute
Run the PCR with the following Cycling Conditions

Results are interpreted by the QuantStudio™ Real-Time PCR Software v1.3 Open the run file and Navigate to Analysis/Analysis Settings/ CT Settings Ensure the following analysis settings are applied Navigate to Analysis/Analysis Settings/Call Settings Click "Edit default Settings" and change the "Quality Value" to 90
